name: Chapatis II
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the flours, salt, and ghee in a large bowl. Gradually add water to form a firm dough, similar to biscuit dough.
2. Knead the dough for 10-15 minutes. The more it is kneaded, the lighter the bread will be.
3. Shape the dough into a ball, cover with plastic wrap, and let rest for at least 1 hour. For best results, let it rest overnight in the refrigerator.
4. When ready to bake, take small pieces of dough (about the size of a golf ball) and roll them on a lightly floured board into thin circles, about 5 inches in diameter. Repeat with all the dough.
5. Heat a griddle or heavy-bottomed iron skillet until very hot. You will see smoke rising from it.
6. Place each rolled-out chapati on the griddle and cook for 2-3 minutes on one side. Using kitchen tongs, check if the underside is golden brown.
7. Flip the chapatis and cook the other side about 1 minute.
8. Remove cooked chapatis to a towel-lined plate. Keep warm in an oven until ready to serve.
9. Serve immediately.
